notable_pick_trades: Acquired 2nd-round pick (No. 38) from Washington, 3rd-round pick (No. 69) from NYG, extra 5th from Cleveland (Howard trade). Sent 4th-round pick (No. 106) and 2027 7th to Detroit (Montgomery trade). Lost 7th rounder in prior deals.
roster_context: Houston went 12-5 with the NFL's No. 1 defense in yards allowed and No. 2 in scoring, but their offense — particularly the offensive line (ranked 30th in PBWR, 32nd in RBWR) and run game (31st in EPA per designed carry) — held them back. C.J. Stroud regressed in his second season and had a disastrous divisional round loss to the Patriots (4 INTs). The Texans are firmly in their Super Bowl window but must dramatically upgrade the offensive line and replace significant defensive line depth lost to free agency. Extensions for Stroud and Will Anderson Jr. loom, tightening cap flexibility (-$13.3M projected). Nick Caserio has already begun reshaping the roster by trading Tytus Howard and acquiring David Montgomery.

needs.explanation: Both starting DTs — Sheldon Rankins (32, UFA) and Tim Settle (UFA) — are free agents. Depth pieces Denico Autry (age 36), Folorunso Fatukasi, and Naquan Jones are also UFAs. Tommy Togiai showed late-season promise and is under contract, but the cupboard is thin. The Texans plan to try to re-sign Rankins on a low-end deal, but need youth to pair alongside Will Anderson Jr. and Danielle Hunter's elite edge rush. Georgia's Christen Miller has been heavily linked to Houston in Round 2-3.
needs.current_starter: Sheldon Rankins (UFA), Tim Settle (UFA) — both potentially gone
needs.depth: Tommy Togiai (under contract, breakout candidate), Kyonte Hamilton (2025 7th-round pick). Very thin if Rankins not re-signed.

needs.explanation: Tim Settle Jr. departed to Washington on a 3-year, $24M deal, creating a hole next to Rankins. Rankins' re-signing (2yr/$14M, $12M gtd) partially addresses this but he is 31 with injury history. Naquan Jones is only a $2M depth signing. Houston needs a quality IDL piece — either a mid-tier FA or a Day 2 draft pick. Need escalated from tier 3 to tier 2.

The interior offensive line is Houston's most glaring need. Starting RG Ed Ingram is an unrestricted free agent, Juice Scruggs was traded to Detroit, and the only IOL starter under contract is C Jake Andrews. The line ranked dead last (32nd) in run block win rate and 30th in pass block win rate. C.J. Stroud has been sacked 113 times in 46 career starts. Multiple mock drafts project Houston selecting an interior lineman in Round 1 (Olaivavega Ioane, Emmanuel Pregnon). The Texans met with Ioane at the Combine.
needs.depth: Scruggs traded away. Extremely thin. No quality IOL depth on roster.

needs.explanation: Azeez Al-Shaair (signed through 2026) and Henry To'oTo'o are the core, but E.J. Speed (UFA), Christian Harris (UFA, fell to 5th-string), and Damone Clark (UFA) are all departing. The linebacker room needs depth and a developmental piece. Speed had some productive snaps but will be 31. The team could use a younger rotational/special teams LB.
needs.depth: Thin after top two. Speed, Harris, Clark all UFAs. Need rotational depth.

needs.explanation: The Texans traded starting RT Tytus Howard to Cleveland and RT Trent Brown is an unrestricted free agent. Only LT Aireontae Ersery (2nd-year, 2025 2nd-round pick) is under contract at tackle. Ersery had the worst run and pass-blocking grades among rookie tackles with 1,200+ snaps. There are whispers he could move to RT, leaving LT wide open. Blake Fisher (backup) is inexperienced. Houston traded away their best offensive lineman for the second straight year.
needs.current_starter: Aireontae Ersery (LT), VACANT (RT — Howard traded, Brown UFA)
needs.depth: Blake Fisher (raw backup), Ersery may switch to RT. Massive need at one tackle spot minimum.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3 (Kadyn Proctor, Blake Miller, Carter Smith linked)

needs.explanation: Christian Kirk departed (rotational loss only). Tank Dell expected back from injury, Jaylin Noel emerging in year 2, Nico Collins locked in. No WR additions made in FA. Need unchanged — Houston could still use a WR2/slot opposite Nico Collins in the mid-rounds of the draft.

Dalton Schultz's 1-year, $12.6M extension retains a starter-caliber tight end who had a career-high 82 catches in 2025. However, the one-year structure means this position is not solved long-term. The depth behind Schultz remains thin. Tier unchanged — the team should still seek a developmental TE in the mid-rounds of the draft.

Will Anderson Jr. (All-Pro, 12 sacks, most pressures in NFL) and Danielle Hunter (All-Pro, 27 combined sacks in two years with Houston) are elite. However, rotational depth behind them is being gutted: Derek Barnett (5 sacks, UFA), Denico Autry (age 36, UFA), and Darrell Taylor are all pending free agents. The team needs a developmental rotational edge rusher to keep Anderson and Hunter fresh. Hunter's extension may not extend beyond 2026, so long-term planning matters.
needs.depth: Derek Barnett (UFA), Denico Autry (UFA), Darrell Taylor (UFA). Rotation depleted.

needs.explanation: The acquisition of David Montgomery from Detroit and the presence of second-year Woody Marks largely addresses this need. Joe Mixon is expected to be released. Montgomery provides a reliable veteran presence, though he turns 29 and his role had decreased in Detroit. Marks averaged only 3.6 YPC behind the poor OL. The run game's issues were more OL-driven than RB-driven. A late-round complementary/explosive back could still help.
needs.current_starter: David Montgomery (acquired via trade), Woody Marks
needs.depth: British Brooks (depth). Montgomery-Marks tandem is serviceable. Mixon likely released.

needs.explanation: Calen Bullock (Pro Bowler, FS) is locked in as a star, but the second safety spot is a concern. M.J. Stewart (starting SS) is a UFA after missing the second half of 2025 with a quad injury. Jimmie Ward was problematic and old, and analysts say he should not return. Jalen Pitre plays a nickel/safety hybrid role but the Texans need a true long-term SS to pair with Bullock. The secondary was elite but faces turnover at this spot.
needs.current_starter: Calen Bullock (FS — locked in), M.J. Stewart (SS — UFA)
needs.depth: Jalen Pitre (nickel/hybrid), Jaylen Reed (2025 6th-round pick). Need a starting-caliber SS.

scheme_fit_notes: DeMeco Ryans runs a 4-3 base with heavy nickel usage. The defense thrives on an aggressive, physical front four that generates pressure up the middle and off the edge. IDL prospects need to be disruptive pass rushers (3-tech penetrators) to complement Anderson and Hunter's edge dominance. On offense, new OC Nick Caley wants to establish a physical run game — IOL prospects with mauling run-blocking ability are prioritized. OT prospects should be capable pass protectors to keep Stroud upright (113 sacks in 46 starts). The secondary plays a lot of man coverage, so any DB additions need man-cover skills and physicality — though the starting CBs (Stingley, Lassiter) and FS (Bullock) are elite and locked in.
